texto: Another cat? Perhaps. For love there is also a season; its seeds must be resown. But a family cat is not replaceable like a wornout coat or a set of tires. Each new kitten becomes its own cat, and none is repeated. I am four cats old, measuring out my life in friends that have succeeded but not replaced one another.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Irving Townsend: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Another cat? Perhaps. For love there is also a season; its seeds must be resown. But a family cat…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, cats, another pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.

texto: We who choose to surround ourselveswith lives even more temporary than ourown, live within a fragile circle;easily and often breached.Unable to accept its awful gaps,we would still live no other way.We cherish memory as the onlycertain immortality, never fullyunderstanding the necessary plan.
